architecture_fit.note: model_forward(batch) is incompatible with task.evaluate's model_fn(input_ids np.ndarray) signature and could never run; injects bias at known ground-truth positions rather than implementing a matching mechanism.

baseline_comparison.note: No strawman or no-bias ablation shown; nothing demonstrates a baseline failing where the method succeeds.

faithfulness.note: Untrained random weights re-instantiated each call plus hand-injected ground-truth attention = pure label leakage, no causal evidence the model uses any circuit.

operating_range.note: Sweep axes exist in task.py but the attempt never executed, so no plen/distance operating-range evidence is produced.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Bias hand-sets the ANSWER (reads source_pos/target_pos labels), not a position-general circuit, so it is not a valid hand-built mechanism.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab reads attn_weights from benchmark.json (which only holds metrics) and no benchmark.json exists; heatmap button is wired to an empty JSON input, so the viz cannot render.

visualisation_rationale.note: README 'Why this visualisation' section is present and plausibly described but disconnected from any working chart or produced result.

notes: The attempt never ran: the only results dir is empty with no benchmark.json, and model_forward's signature is incompatible with task.evaluate. The core idea injects a +2.0 attention bias at the known ground-truth (target_pos, source_pos) pair, which is label leakage rather than a substring-matching mechanism, and the model uses untrained random weights re-created every forward call. Fails on architecture fit, baseline comparison, and faithfulness.
